depression.description: I often feel like I'm trapped in my own body. My physical pain makes me feel helpless, and I worry that I'll never be able to live life fully again.
I struggle with the belief that my health issues make me unlovable. I constantly worry that my partner will leave me if they see how sick I truly am.
I feel like I have to put on a brave face around others. If I show my true feelings, I fear they won't want to be around me anymore.
When I receive bad news about my health, like the MRI report, I feel completely overwhelmed. I can't shake the thought that my situation will never improve.
At social gatherings, I can't help but compare myself to others. I see them looking healthy and happy, and it makes me feel even more isolated and sad.

Delusion.description: I often think that my worth is tied to my health. If I'm not healthy, I feel like I'm not deserving of love or friendship.

Delusion.topic: Fear of abandonment due to illness
Delusion.description: I have this constant fear that my partner will abandon me when they see how much I struggle with my health. It makes me hesitant to open up to them.
